rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHORBA VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, Line 1, PETITIONER JOHN CHORBA MD's Motion For Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Hearing required re status of administrative record, hearing schedule. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. DEJA VU - SAN FRANCISCO, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, Line 2, DEFENDANT SFBSC MANAGEMENT, LLC's Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons. Defendant SFBSC Management, LLC's motion to dismiss for delay in service of summons is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB HARKER ET AL VS. JOEL FINK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 3, 2024, Line 9, DEFENDANTS JEAN CHA , AN INDIVIDUAL, EDWIN SASAKI, and CHA LAW ETHIC's Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees. Defendants Jean Cha, Cha Law Ethics and Edwin Sasaki's (defendants') motion for attorney fees is granted in the amount of $16,280. Defendants filed and won a CCP 425.16 anti-SLAPP motion against counts 6 (UCL) and 8 (unjust enrichment) of the complaint. As plaintiffs concede, "attorney's fees to a prevailing defendant on an Anti-SLAPP motion are mandatory." (Opp. 3:28-29.) Defendants won everything their motion sought and thus prevailed. Plaintiffs also concede defendants' challenges to the two counts "may not have technically been SLAPPbacks," after suggesting they were. (Opp. 3:6-26.) The $400 hourly rate is low by local standards, and defendants' briefing was well-written and persuasive. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
